Minimal adjustment: (0.22 * 0.6) + (0.26 * 0.4) = 0.24 | [mean_reversion] At 26%, market reasonably prices Rayner's chances. title: Will Restore Britain win the next U.K. election?
reasoning: [bayesian] Market at 8% still seems too high. Restore Britain appears to be a minor political party in the UK. The UK's electoral system strongly favors established parties (Conservative, Labour, Lib Dem). New or minor parties face enormous structural barriers to winning a general election. Historical precedent shows such outcomes are extremely rare in established democracies with FPTP systems. | [contrarian] Market pricing seems roughly appropriate. Small parties face structural barriers in UK system. However, potential for political volatility and protest votes if major parties continue struggling. 8% might slightly underweight tail risk of major political realignment, but not significantly mispriced. | [base_rate] Reference class: New/minor party winning national election. Historical base rate extremely low (~2-5%). Restore Britain appears to be minor party. Market at 8% already reflects low probability. Modest upward adjustment from base rate, weighted with market price. | [mean_reversion] Market at 8% may be too pessimistic. UK politics highly volatile - Brexit, economic crises have created space for new movements. 4+ years allows for significant political realignment. While Restore Britain is currently minor, extreme political shifts can happen rapidly in unstable environments. Fat tail scenario underpriced.

title: What will the price of GTA VI be?
reasoning: [bayesian] This appears to be asking if GTA VI will cost $100+. Current AAA games max at $70-80. Even with inflation over 5+ years, $100+ would be unprecedented and likely face massive consumer backlash. Market at 12% still seems high. | [contrarian] Market anchored to traditional $60-70 AAA pricing despite inflation and GTA's premium positioning. Take-Two has pricing power with this franchise - GTA V generated $8B+ over decade. $100+ price point increasingly normalized for premium games. Market underweighting publisher incentives and consumer willingness to pay for marquee titles. | [base_rate] Reference class: AAA game pricing at $100+ threshold. Market price 12% gets 40% weight. | [mean_reversion] At 12%, market may be too pessimistic. Gaming prices have inflated significantly, and GTA is premium franchise. $100+ price point becoming more common for AAA titles with special editions. Tail risk of extreme pricing exists.
